user_prompt: Draw a coordinate plane with a circle centered at the origin O. Pick a point (x, y) on the circle and draw a right triangle with the right angle at point F = (x, 0) on the x-axis. The legs go from O to F along the x-axis (length x) and from F straight up to the point (x, y) (length y), and the hypotenuse goes from O to (x, y) — that's the radius r. Label all three sides x, y, and r, and mark the right angle at F. Write x² + y² = r² next to the triangle.
